title: lora_l2_tx holds mutex during blocking lora_send - potential RX starvation
description:
File: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:253-269

lichen_lora_l2_tx() holds lora_mutex while calling lora_send(), which blocks until TX completes. At SF10/125kHz, a 255-byte packet takes ~500ms airtime.

Meanwhile, the RX thread also acquires lora_mutex (line 127) to snapshot the callback. If TX is in progress, the RX thread blocks waiting for mutex, but it cannot deliver packets until TX releases.

Trigger: Transmit a large packet while another node is sending.

Bad behavior: Received packets are delayed by TX airtime (~500ms). In half-duplex LoRa radios, this may cause packet loss if radio switches to TX during incoming reception.

Note: The mutex protects running state, but the lora_send itself does not need serialization if the driver handles it. Consider narrowing the critical section to just the state check.
close_reason: Fixed: removed mutex from lichen_lora_l2_tx(). The atomic_t running field is read directly without locking, allowing RX thread to proceed during TX. Added detailed comment explaining why this is safe (atomic read, Zephyr driver thread-safety, driver handles stop race).
